[PRIVATE_URL_WITH_CREDENTIALS] option ForwardAgent yes will allow us to perform SSH key forwarding.

  1. Since access to this bastion will be quite frequent, to avoid entering a password, we will add our public key to the authorized keys on this machine:

    1. Enter the bastion using ssh bastion-bancard and our password.

    2. Create (if it doesn't exist) the folder ~/.ssh with the command mkdir ~/.ssh.

    3. Create (if it doesn't exist) the file ~/.ssh/authorized_keys using the command vim ~/.ssh/authorized_keys and add our public SSH key to it, which can be found in the file ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub on our machine.

    4. We modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh folder using the command chmod 700 ~/.ssh and of the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file on the bastion using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys.

By following these steps, we should now be able to SSH into the development bastion using the command ssh bastion-bancard, without requiring a password.

Deployment Server

IP: 10.100.14.57

Access from: Bastión de desarrollo

User: servicios
Password:[PASSWORD] Bancard2020$

The deployment server is the machine used for deploying both staging and production environments for all Bancard services and portals. Access to this machine will be done through the development bastion with key forwarding.

Recommended Configuration

  1. Access the development bastion with the command ssh bastion-bancard.

  2. Create the host for the deployment server on the bastion:

    1. Create the file ~/.ssh/config using the command vim ~/.ssh/config and add the following host:

    2. This will allow us to access the deployment server using the command ssh deploy-server instead of ssh services@10.100.14.57.

    3. Modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh/config file using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config.

  3. To avoid password prompts when accessing the deployment server, we need to add our public key to the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file on this machine. To do this, we should enter it with ssh deploy-server, open the file with vim ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, and paste our public key inside it, the same key we added inside the bastion, which can be found in the ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub file on our machine.

Staging Proxy

IP: 10.100.14.73

Access from: Bastión de desarrollo

User: IPA

This machine is responsible for performing proxy pass to all staging services. Access to this machine will be done through the development bastion with key forwarding.

Recommended Configuration

  1. Enter the development bastion.

  2. Create a host for the proxy server on the bastion:

    1. Open the ~/.ssh/config file with the command vim ~/.ssh/config and add the following hosts:

      ]]>

    2. This will allow us to access the staging proxy using the command: ssh staging-proxy.

    3. If you haven't already done so in the previous part, modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh/config file using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config.

  3. To avoid password prompts when entering the staging proxy, we need to add our public key to this machine's authorized keys:

    1. Access the machine with ssh staging-proxy and our password.

    2. Create (if it doesn't exist) the ~/.ssh folder using the command mkdir ~/.ssh.

    3. Create (if it doesn't exist) the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file using the command vim ~/.ssh/authorized_keys and add our public SSH key to it, which can be found in the ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub file on our machine.

    4. Modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh folder using the command chmod 700 ~/.ssh and of the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file on the bastion using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys.

Staging Servers (with dockerized services/portals)

Access from: Bastión de desarrollo

User: IPA

The recommended configuration and access will be the same for all staging servers. Access to these machines will be done through the development bastion with key forwarding. We will use the server where auth, commerce, epos, mpos, and vpos are located as an example.

Recommended Configuration

  1. Enter the development bastion.

  2. Create hosts for the servers on the bastion:

    1. Open the ~/.ssh/config file with the command vim ~/.ssh/config and add the following host:

      ]]>

    2. This will allow us to access the server with the following commands: ssh auth-srv, ssh commerce-srv, ssh epos-srv, etc.

    3. If you haven't done so already in the previous part, modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh/config file using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/config.

  3. To avoid password prompts when entering the servers, we need to add our public key to the authorized keys on these machines:

    1. We access the server with ssh [auth-srv|commerce-srv|...] and our password.

    2. Create (if it doesn't exist) the ~/.ssh folder using the command mkdir ~/.ssh.

    3. Create (if it doesn't exist) the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file using the command vim ~/.ssh/authorized_keys and add our public SSH key to it, which can be found in the ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub file on our machine.

    4. Modify the access permissions of the ~/.ssh folder using the command chmod 700 ~/.ssh, and of the ~/.ssh/authorized_keys file on the bastion using the command chmod 600 ~/.ssh/authorized_keys.

  4. In order to use Docker on the staging machines, we need to add our user to the docker group: sudo usermod -aG docker <ipa-user>. To apply this change, we must log out of the server and log back in.

  5. Return to the development bastion and repeat steps 1 to 4 for the rest of the servers where dockerized services are located.

MISSION ACCOMPLISHED!

Annex

Example of ~/.ssh/config file on the development bastion:

## STAGING SERVICES ## host auth-srv commerce-srv epos-srv mpos-srv vpos-srv   hostname 10.100.14.23   user host payment-link-srv   hostname 10.100.14.113   user host billing-srv external-commerce-srv mock-srv notification-srv reversal-srv selling-srv transfer-srv   hostname 10.100.14.114   user ## STAGING PORTALS ## host auth-ptl commerce-suite payment-link-ptl selling-ptl vpos-ptl   hostname 192.168.128.65   user ]]>
